repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax — banked finite monotonicity-loss and fixed-record diagnostic substrate.

published_prior_art.cite: RosenbaumKrieger1990 gives ordered one-sided extrema; ZhangSmallHeng2024 records their rank-monotone binary-exposure characterization and a related hardness boundary, but neither states this opposite-tail union witness.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax — banked Gamma=1 equality regression fixture.

published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum1987SensitivityPermutation — Gamma=1 reduces the assignment law to uniformity; reversal equality is an elementary symmetry specialization.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax — banked d-dagger gain 19/120 regression fixture.

published_prior_art.cite: none — checked published comparators do not state this signed opposite-tail matched-set fixture.

repo_prior_art.cite: stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax reserves the practical exact joint-calibration and strictness-cell problem as a follow-on gap.

published_prior_art.cite: Rosenbaum2018Stratified is conservative finite-sample Taylor/separable calibration; CohenOlsonFogarty2020AdversarialGame is asymptotic and multivariate one-sided; ChiuKang2025Contingency is exact but for contingency-table permutation-invariant tests.

novelty_flags.label: Theorem 2 (thm:gamma-one-equality)
novelty_flags.one_line: The Gamma=1 equality is already banked in stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax and should remain only a credited sanity/regression result.

novelty_flags.label: Proposition 1 (prop:gamma-two-gap-witness)
novelty_flags.one_line: The d-dagger gain 19/120 is already banked in stat_rosenbaum_lf_family_minimax and cannot supply new kernel novelty.

soundness_flags.label: def:local-rosenbaum-vertices
soundness_flags.one_line: For Gamma>1 the included H={1,...,m_i} produces the uniform interior point, not a local polytope vertex; restrict H to nonempty proper subsets or rename this set as candidate assignment vectors.

soundness_flags.label: def:robust-two-tail-calibration and def:strict-improvement-region
soundness_flags.one_line: p_B=2 min(p_+,p_-) is signless despite p_+ and p_- differing under hidden bias, so it is not a direction-aware conventional two-sided sensitivity p-value; make it depend on the observed sign or explicitly treat it as a non-valid numerical comparator and withdraw reporting-validity language.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / Fisher-sharp-null model
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Calibration is conditional on the Fisher sharp null and the conditional Rosenbaum product-law class, not weak-null or heterogeneous-effect validity.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Exact shared-law two-tail calibration under the Fisher-sharp-null Rosenbaum product-law model.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Open-ended Question 1 / computational domain
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Termination and verification are limited to finitely encoded rational scores, alpha, and Gamma, with no polynomial-time guarantee.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Finite exact-rational certification on the declared encoded-input domain, potentially with exponential runtime.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Replace the signless doubled-minimum comparator with a sign-indexed conventional procedure, then state and compare both procedures' valid rejection regions under the Rosenbaum model.
Define local Rosenbaum vertices using nonempty proper high sets and separately include the uniform law only where it is needed as a candidate.
Strengthen JPC beyond depth-first exhaustive enumeration with a substantive score-state, parameter-cell, or certificate-compression guarantee that remains meaningful despite the stated #P-hardness.

recommended_next_step: Revise the invalid/ambiguous doubled-tail comparator and the local-vertex definition, retain banked Gamma fixtures solely as sanity checks, then re-run novelty review.
